title: cuphea × speciosa
intro: Cuphea × speciosa, also known as firecracker plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lythraceae. The plant is known for its vibrant red flowers, which grow in clusters and resemble small fireworks, hence its common name. Cuphea × speciosa has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant's health benefits include reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting immune function, and it can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion for medicinal use.
